item.name: Silvered Arrow
item.text:
Some monsters that have immunity or resistance to nonmagical weapons are susceptible to silver ammunition, so cautious adventurers invest extra coin to plate their ammunition in silver. You can silver ten pieces of ammunition for 100 gp. This cost represents not only the price of the silver, but the time and expertise needed to add silver to the ammunition without making it less effective.
Ammunition: You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. The ammunition of a firearm is destroyed upon use. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Silvered Battleaxe
item.text:
Some monsters that have immunity or resistance to nonmagical weapons are susceptible to silver weapons, so cautious adventurers invest extra coin to plate their weapons with silver. You can silver a single weapon for 100 gp. This cost represents not only the price of the silver, but the time and expertise needed to add silver to the weapon without making it less effective.
Versatile: This weapon can be used with one or two hands. A damage value in parentheses appears with the property—the damage when the weapon is used with two hands to make a melee attack.
Proficiency: martial, battleaxe Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Silvered Club
item.text:
Some monsters that have immunity or resistance to nonmagical weapons are susceptible to silver weapons, so cautious adventurers invest extra coin to plate their weapons with silver. You can silver a single weapon for 100 gp. This cost represents not only the price of the silver, but the time and expertise needed to add silver to the weapon without making it less effective.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: simple, club Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Silvered Dagger
item.text:
Some monsters that have immunity or resistance to nonmagical weapons are susceptible to silver weapons, so cautious adventurers invest extra coin to plate their weapons with silver. You can silver a single weapon for 100 gp. This cost represents not only the price of the silver, but the time and expertise needed to add silver to the weapon without making it less effective.
Finesse: When making an attack with a finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Thrown: If a weapon has the Thrown property, you can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack. If the weapon is a melee weapon, you use the same ability modifier for that attack roll and damage roll that you would use for a melee attack with the weapon. For example, if you throw a handaxe, you use your Strength, but if you throw a dagger, you can use either your Strength or your Dexterity, since the dagger has the Finesse property.
Range: A weapon that can be used to make a ranged attack has a range shown in parentheses after the ammunition or thrown property. The range lists two numbers. The first is the weapon's normal range in feet, and the second indicates the weapon's maximum range. When attacking a target beyond normal range, you have disadvantage on the attack roll. You can't attack a target beyond the weapon's long range.
Proficiency: simple, dagger Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Silvered Flail
item.text:
Some monsters that have immunity or resistance to nonmagical weapons are susceptible to silver weapons, so cautious adventurers invest extra coin to plate their weapons with silver. You can silver a single weapon for 100 gp. This cost represents not only the price of the silver, but the time and expertise needed to add silver to the weapon without making it less effective.
Proficiency: martial, flail Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Silvered Glaive
item.text:
Some monsters that have immunity or resistance to nonmagical weapons are susceptible to silver weapons, so cautious adventurers invest extra coin to plate their weapons with silver. You can silver a single weapon for 100 gp. This cost represents not only the price of the silver, but the time and expertise needed to add silver to the weapon without making it less effective.
Heavy: Creatures that are Small or Tiny have disadvantage on attack rolls with heavy weapons. A heavy weapon's size and bulk make it too large for a Small or Tiny creature to use effectively.
Reach: This weapon adds 5 feet to your reach when you attack with it, as well as when determining your reach for opportunity attacks with it.
Two-Handed: This weapon requires two hands when you attack with it.
Proficiency: martial, glaive Source: System Reference Document 5.0

Heavy: Creatures that are Small or Tiny have disadvantage on attack rolls with heavy weapons. A heavy weapon's size and bulk make it too large for a Small or Tiny creature to use effectively.

Proficiency: martial, greataxe Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.text:

Special: You have disadvantage when you use a lance to attack a target within 5 feet of you. Also, a lance requires two hands to wield when you aren't mounted.

Proficiency: martial, lance Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Silvered Whip
item.text:

Finesse: When making an attack with a finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.
Reach: This weapon adds 5 feet to your reach when you attack with it, as well as when determining your reach for opportunity attacks with it.
Proficiency: martial, whip Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Adamantine Breastplate
item.text:
This suit of armor is reinforced with adamantine, one of the hardest substances in existence. While you're wearing it, any critical hit against you becomes a normal hit.
This armor consists of a fitted metal chest piece worn with supple leather. Although it leaves the legs and arms relatively unprotected, this armor provides good protection for the wearer's vital organs while leaving the wearer relatively unencumbered.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Adamantine Chain Mail
item.text:
This suit of armor is reinforced with adamantine, one of the hardest substances in existence. While you're wearing it, any critical hit against you becomes a normal hit.
Made of interlocking metal rings, chain mail includes a layer of quilted fabric worn underneath the mail to prevent chafing and to cushion the impact of blows. The suit includes gauntlets.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
If the wearer has a Strength score lower than 13, their speed is reduced by 10 feet.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Adamantine Chain Shirt
item.text:
This suit of armor is reinforced with adamantine, one of the hardest substances in existence. While you're wearing it, any critical hit against you becomes a normal hit.
Made of interlocking metal rings, a chain shirt is worn between layers of clothing or leather. This armor offers modest protection to the wearer's upper body and allows the sound of the rings rubbing against one another to be muffled by outer layers.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Adamantine Half Plate Armor
item.text:
This suit of armor is reinforced with adamantine, one of the hardest substances in existence. While you're wearing it, any critical hit against you becomes a normal hit.
Half plate consists of shaped metal plates that cover most of the wearer's body. It does not include leg protection beyond simple greaves that are attached with leather straps.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Adamantine Plate Armor
item.text:
This suit of armor is reinforced with adamantine, one of the hardest substances in existence. While you're wearing it, any critical hit against you becomes a normal hit.
Plate consists of shaped, interlocking metal plates to cover the entire body. A suit of plate includes gauntlets, heavy leather boots, a visored helmet, and thick layers of padding underneath the armor. Buckles and straps distribute the weight over the body.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
If the wearer has a Strength score lower than 15, their speed is reduced by 10 feet.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Adamantine Ring Mail
item.text:
This suit of armor is reinforced with adamantine, one of the hardest substances in existence. While you're wearing it, any critical hit against you becomes a normal hit.
This armor is leather armor with heavy rings sewn into it. The rings help reinforce the armor against blows from swords and axes. Ring mail is inferior to chain mail, and it's usually worn only by those who can't afford better armor.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Adamantine Scale Mail
item.text:
This suit of armor is reinforced with adamantine, one of the hardest substances in existence. While you're wearing it, any critical hit against you becomes a normal hit.
This armor consists of a coat and leggings (and perhaps a separate skirt) of leather covered with overlapping pieces of metal, much like the scales of a fish. The suit includes gauntlets.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Adamantine Splint Armor
item.text:
This suit of armor is reinforced with adamantine, one of the hardest substances in existence. While you're wearing it, any critical hit against you becomes a normal hit.
This armor is made of narrow vertical strips of metal riveted to a backing of leather that is worn over cloth padding. Flexible chain mail protects the joints.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
If the wearer has a Strength score lower than 15, their speed is reduced by 10 feet.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Arrow +1
item.text:
You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this piece of magic ammunition. Once it hits a target, the ammunition is no longer magical.
Ammunition: You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. The ammunition of a firearm is destroyed upon use. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Arrows +1 (20)
item.text:
You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this piece of magic ammunition. Once it hits a target, the ammunition is no longer magical.
Ammunition: You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. The ammunition of a firearm is destroyed upon use. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Sling Bullets +1 (20)
item.text:
You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this piece of magic ammunition. Once it hits a target, the ammunition is no longer magical.
Ammunition: You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. The ammunition of a firearm is destroyed upon use. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Breastplate +1
item.text:
You have a +1 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.
This armor consists of a fitted metal chest piece worn with supple leather. Although it leaves the legs and arms relatively unprotected, this armor provides good protection for the wearer's vital organs while leaving the wearer relatively unencumbered.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Chain Mail +1
item.text:
You have a +1 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.
Made of interlocking metal rings, chain mail includes a layer of quilted fabric worn underneath the mail to prevent chafing and to cushion the impact of blows. The suit includes gauntlets.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
If the wearer has a Strength score lower than 13, their speed is reduced by 10 feet.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Chain Shirt +1
item.text:
You have a +1 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.
Made of interlocking metal rings, a chain shirt is worn between layers of clothing or leather. This armor offers modest protection to the wearer's upper body and allows the sound of the rings rubbing against one another to be muffled by outer layers.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Half Plate Armor +1
item.text:
You have a +1 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.
Half plate consists of shaped metal plates that cover most of the wearer's body. It does not include leg protection beyond simple greaves that are attached with leather straps.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Hide Armor +1
item.text:
You have a +1 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.
This crude armor consists of thick furs and pelts. It is commonly worn by barbarian tribes, evil humanoids, and other folk who lack access to the tools and materials needed to create better armor.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Leather Armor +1
item.text:
You have a +1 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.
The breastplate and shoulder protectors of this armor are made of leather that has been stiffened by being boiled in oil. The rest of the armor is made of softer and more flexible materials.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Padded Armor +1
item.text:
You have a +1 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.
Padded armor consists of quilted layers of cloth and batting.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Plate Armor +1
item.text:
You have a +1 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.
Plate consists of shaped, interlocking metal plates to cover the entire body. A suit of plate includes gauntlets, heavy leather boots, a visored helmet, and thick layers of padding underneath the armor. Buckles and straps distribute the weight over the body.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
If the wearer has a Strength score lower than 15, their speed is reduced by 10 feet.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Ring Mail +1
item.text:
You have a +1 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.
This armor is leather armor with heavy rings sewn into it. The rings help reinforce the armor against blows from swords and axes. Ring mail is inferior to chain mail, and it's usually worn only by those who can't afford better armor.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Scale Mail +1
item.text:
You have a +1 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.
This armor consists of a coat and leggings (and perhaps a separate skirt) of leather covered with overlapping pieces of metal, much like the scales of a fish. The suit includes gauntlets.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Splint Armor +1
item.text:
You have a +1 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.
This armor is made of narrow vertical strips of metal riveted to a backing of leather that is worn over cloth padding. Flexible chain mail protects the joints.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
If the wearer has a Strength score lower than 15, their speed is reduced by 10 feet.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Studded Leather Armor +1
item.text:
You have a +1 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.
Made from tough but flexible leather, studded leather is reinforced with close-set rivets or spikes.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Shield +1
item.text:
While holding this shield, you have a +1 bonus to AC. This bonus is in addition to the shield's normal bonus to AC.
A shield is made from wood or metal and is carried in one hand. Wielding a shield increases your Armor Class by 2. You can benefit from only one shield at a time.
Proficiency: shield Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Battleaxe +1
item.text:
You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Versatile: This weapon can be used with one or two hands. A damage value in parentheses appears with the property—the damage when the weapon is used with two hands to make a melee attack.
Proficiency: martial, battleaxe Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Blowgun +1
item.text:
Ammo: blowgun needle
You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Ammunition: You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. The ammunition of a firearm is destroyed upon use. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield. If you use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a melee attack, you treat the weapon as an improvised weapon (see "Improvised Weapons").
Range: A weapon that can be used to make a ranged attack has a range shown in parentheses after the ammunition or thrown property. The range lists two numbers. The first is the weapon's normal range in feet, and the second indicates the weapon's maximum range. When attacking a target beyond normal range, you have disadvantage on the attack roll. You can't attack a target beyond the weapon's long range.
Loading: Because of the time required to load this weapon, you can fire only one piece of ammunition from it when you use an action, bonus action, or reaction to fire it, regardless of the number of attacks you can normally make.
Proficiency: martial, blowgun Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Club +1
item.text:
You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: simple, club Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Dagger +1
item.text:
You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Finesse: When making an attack with a finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Thrown: If a weapon has the Thrown property, you can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack. If the weapon is a melee weapon, you use the same ability modifier for that attack roll and damage roll that you would use for a melee attack with the weapon. For example, if you throw a handaxe, you use your Strength, but if you throw a dagger, you can use either your Strength or your Dexterity, since the dagger has the Finesse property.
Range: A weapon that can be used to make a ranged attack has a range shown in parentheses after the ammunition or thrown property. The range lists two numbers. The first is the weapon's normal range in feet, and the second indicates the weapon's maximum range. When attacking a target beyond normal range, you have disadvantage on the attack roll. You can't attack a target beyond the weapon's long range.
Proficiency: simple, dagger Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Dart +1
item.text:
You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Finesse: When making an attack with a finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.
Thrown: If a weapon has the Thrown property, you can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack. If the weapon is a melee weapon, you use the same ability modifier for that attack roll and damage roll that you would use for a melee attack with the weapon. For example, if you throw a handaxe, you use your Strength, but if you throw a dagger, you can use either your Strength or your Dexterity, since the dagger has the Finesse property.
Range: A weapon that can be used to make a ranged attack has a range shown in parentheses after the ammunition or thrown property. The range lists two numbers. The first is the weapon's normal range in feet, and the second indicates the weapon's maximum range. When attacking a target beyond normal range, you have disadvantage on the attack roll. You can't attack a target beyond the weapon's long range.
Proficiency: simple, dart Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Flail +1
item.text:
You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Proficiency: martial, flail Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Glaive +1
item.text:
You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Heavy: Creatures that are Small or Tiny have disadvantage on attack rolls with heavy weapons. A heavy weapon's size and bulk make it too large for a Small or Tiny creature to use effectively.
Reach: This weapon adds 5 feet to your reach when you attack with it, as well as when determining your reach for opportunity attacks with it.
Two-Handed: This weapon requires two hands when you attack with it.
Proficiency: martial, glaive Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Greataxe +1
item.text:
You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Heavy: Creatures that are Small or Tiny have disadvantage on attack rolls with heavy weapons. A heavy weapon's size and bulk make it too large for a Small or Tiny creature to use effectively.
Two-Handed: This weapon requires two hands when you attack with it.
Proficiency: martial, greataxe Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.text:
You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Two-Handed: This weapon requires two hands when you attack with it.
Proficiency: simple, greatclub Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Lance +1
item.text:
You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Special: You have disadvantage when you use a lance to attack a target within 5 feet of you. Also, a lance requires two hands to wield when you aren't mounted.
Reach: This weapon adds 5 feet to your reach when you attack with it, as well as when determining your reach for opportunity attacks with it.
Proficiency: martial, lance Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Morningstar +1
item.text:
You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Proficiency: martial, morningstar Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Net +1
item.text:
You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Special: A Large or smaller creature hit by a net is restrained until it is freed. A net has no effect on creatures that are formless, or creatures that are Huge or larger. A creature can use its action to make a DC 10 Strength check, freeing itself or another creature within its reach on a success. Dealing 5 slashing damage to the net (AC 10) also frees the creature without harming it, ending the effect and destroying the net. When you use an action, bonus action, or reaction to attack with a net, you can make only one attack regardless of the number of attacks you can normally make.
Thrown: If a weapon has the Thrown property, you can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack. If the weapon is a melee weapon, you use the same ability modifier for that attack roll and damage roll that you would use for a melee attack with the weapon. For example, if you throw a handaxe, you use your Strength, but if you throw a dagger, you can use either your Strength or your Dexterity, since the dagger has the Finesse property.
Range: A weapon that can be used to make a ranged attack has a range shown in parentheses after the ammunition or thrown property. The range lists two numbers. The first is the weapon's normal range in feet, and the second indicates the weapon's maximum range. When attacking a target beyond normal range, you have disadvantage on the attack roll. You can't attack a target beyond the weapon's long range.
Proficiency: martial, net Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.text:

Heavy: Creatures that are Small or Tiny have disadvantage on attack rolls with heavy weapons. A heavy weapon's size and bulk make it too large for a Small or Tiny creature to use effectively.

item.text:
You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Finesse: When making an attack with a finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: martial, scimitar Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Shortbow +1
item.text:
Ammo: arrow
You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Ammunition: You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. The ammunition of a firearm is destroyed upon use. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield. If you use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a melee attack, you treat the weapon as an improvised weapon (see "Improvised Weapons").
Range: A weapon that can be used to make a ranged attack has a range shown in parentheses after the ammunition or thrown property. The range lists two numbers. The first is the weapon's normal range in feet, and the second indicates the weapon's maximum range. When attacking a target beyond normal range, you have disadvantage on the attack roll. You can't attack a target beyond the weapon's long range.
Two-Handed: This weapon requires two hands when you attack with it.
Proficiency: simple, shortbow Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Shortsword +1
item.text:
You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Finesse: When making an attack with a finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: martial, shortsword Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Sickle +1
item.text:
You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: simple, sickle Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Sling +1
item.text:
Ammo: sling bullet
You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Ammunition: You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. The ammunition of a firearm is destroyed upon use. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield. If you use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a melee attack, you treat the weapon as an improvised weapon (see "Improvised Weapons"). A sling must be loaded to deal any damage when used in this way.
Range: A weapon that can be used to make a ranged attack has a range shown in parentheses after the ammunition or thrown property. The range lists two numbers. The first is the weapon's normal range in feet, and the second indicates the weapon's maximum range. When attacking a target beyond normal range, you have disadvantage on the attack roll. You can't attack a target beyond the weapon's long range.
Proficiency: simple, sling Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Spear +1
item.text:
You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Thrown: If a weapon has the Thrown property, you can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack. If the weapon is a melee weapon, you use the same ability modifier for that attack roll and damage roll that you would use for a melee attack with the weapon. For example, if you throw a handaxe, you use your Strength, but if you throw a dagger, you can use either your Strength or your Dexterity, since the dagger has the Finesse property.
Range: A weapon that can be used to make a ranged attack has a range shown in parentheses after the ammunition or thrown property. The range lists two numbers. The first is the weapon's normal range in feet, and the second indicates the weapon's maximum range. When attacking a target beyond normal range, you have disadvantage on the attack roll. You can't attack a target beyond the weapon's long range.
Versatile: This weapon can be used with one or two hands. A damage value in parentheses appears with the property—the damage when the weapon is used with two hands to make a melee attack.
Proficiency: simple, spear Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Trident +1
item.text:
You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Thrown: If a weapon has the Thrown property, you can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack. If the weapon is a melee weapon, you use the same ability modifier for that attack roll and damage roll that you would use for a melee attack with the weapon. For example, if you throw a handaxe, you use your Strength, but if you throw a dagger, you can use either your Strength or your Dexterity, since the dagger has the Finesse property.
Range: A weapon that can be used to make a ranged attack has a range shown in parentheses after the ammunition or thrown property. The range lists two numbers. The first is the weapon's normal range in feet, and the second indicates the weapon's maximum range. When attacking a target beyond normal range, you have disadvantage on the attack roll. You can't attack a target beyond the weapon's long range.
Versatile: This weapon can be used with one or two hands. A damage value in parentheses appears with the property—the damage when the weapon is used with two hands to make a melee attack.
Proficiency: martial, trident Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: War Pick +1
item.text:
You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Proficiency: martial, war pick Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Warhammer +1
item.text:
You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Versatile: This weapon can be used with one or two hands. A damage value in parentheses appears with the property—the damage when the weapon is used with two hands to make a melee attack.
Proficiency: martial, warhammer Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Whip +1
item.text:
You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Finesse: When making an attack with a finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.
Reach: This weapon adds 5 feet to your reach when you attack with it, as well as when determining your reach for opportunity attacks with it.
Proficiency: martial, whip Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Arrow +2
item.text:
You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this piece of magic ammunition. Once it hits a target, the ammunition is no longer magical.
Ammunition: You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. The ammunition of a firearm is destroyed upon use. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Sling Bullets +2 (20)
item.text:
You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this piece of magic ammunition. Once it hits a target, the ammunition is no longer magical.
Ammunition: You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. The ammunition of a firearm is destroyed upon use. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Breastplate +2
item.text:
You have a +2 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.
This armor consists of a fitted metal chest piece worn with supple leather. Although it leaves the legs and arms relatively unprotected, this armor provides good protection for the wearer's vital organs while leaving the wearer relatively unencumbered.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Chain Mail +2
item.text:
You have a +2 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.
Made of interlocking metal rings, chain mail includes a layer of quilted fabric worn underneath the mail to prevent chafing and to cushion the impact of blows. The suit includes gauntlets.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
If the wearer has a Strength score lower than 13, their speed is reduced by 10 feet.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Chain Shirt +2
item.text:
You have a +2 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.
Made of interlocking metal rings, a chain shirt is worn between layers of clothing or leather. This armor offers modest protection to the wearer's upper body and allows the sound of the rings rubbing against one another to be muffled by outer layers.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Half Plate Armor +2
item.text:
You have a +2 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.
Half plate consists of shaped metal plates that cover most of the wearer's body. It does not include leg protection beyond simple greaves that are attached with leather straps.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Hide Armor +2
item.text:
You have a +2 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.
This crude armor consists of thick furs and pelts. It is commonly worn by barbarian tribes, evil humanoids, and other folk who lack access to the tools and materials needed to create better armor.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Leather Armor +2
item.text:
You have a +2 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.
The breastplate and shoulder protectors of this armor are made of leather that has been stiffened by being boiled in oil. The rest of the armor is made of softer and more flexible materials.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Padded Armor +2
item.text:
You have a +2 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.
Padded armor consists of quilted layers of cloth and batting.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Plate Armor +2
item.text:
You have a +2 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.
Plate consists of shaped, interlocking metal plates to cover the entire body. A suit of plate includes gauntlets, heavy leather boots, a visored helmet, and thick layers of padding underneath the armor. Buckles and straps distribute the weight over the body.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
If the wearer has a Strength score lower than 15, their speed is reduced by 10 feet.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Ring Mail +2
item.text:
You have a +2 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.
This armor is leather armor with heavy rings sewn into it. The rings help reinforce the armor against blows from swords and axes. Ring mail is inferior to chain mail, and it's usually worn only by those who can't afford better armor.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Scale Mail +2
item.text:
You have a +2 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.
This armor consists of a coat and leggings (and perhaps a separate skirt) of leather covered with overlapping pieces of metal, much like the scales of a fish. The suit includes gauntlets.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Splint Armor +2
item.text:
You have a +2 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.
This armor is made of narrow vertical strips of metal riveted to a backing of leather that is worn over cloth padding. Flexible chain mail protects the joints.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
If the wearer has a Strength score lower than 15, their speed is reduced by 10 feet.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Studded Leather Armor +2
item.text:
You have a +2 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.
Made from tough but flexible leather, studded leather is reinforced with close-set rivets or spikes.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Shield +2
item.text:
While holding this shield, you have a +2 bonus to AC. This bonus is in addition to the shield's normal bonus to AC.
A shield is made from wood or metal and is carried in one hand. Wielding a shield increases your Armor Class by 2. You can benefit from only one shield at a time.
Proficiency: shield Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Battleaxe +2
item.text:
You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Versatile: This weapon can be used with one or two hands. A damage value in parentheses appears with the property—the damage when the weapon is used with two hands to make a melee attack.
Proficiency: martial, battleaxe Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Blowgun +2
item.text:
Ammo: blowgun needle
You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Ammunition: You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. The ammunition of a firearm is destroyed upon use. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield. If you use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a melee attack, you treat the weapon as an improvised weapon (see "Improvised Weapons").
Range: A weapon that can be used to make a ranged attack has a range shown in parentheses after the ammunition or thrown property. The range lists two numbers. The first is the weapon's normal range in feet, and the second indicates the weapon's maximum range. When attacking a target beyond normal range, you have disadvantage on the attack roll. You can't attack a target beyond the weapon's long range.
Loading: Because of the time required to load this weapon, you can fire only one piece of ammunition from it when you use an action, bonus action, or reaction to fire it, regardless of the number of attacks you can normally make.
Proficiency: martial, blowgun Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.text:
You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Heavy: Creatures that are Small or Tiny have disadvantage on attack rolls with heavy weapons. A heavy weapon's size and bulk make it too large for a Small or Tiny creature to use effectively.

item.text:
You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

item.name: Light Hammer +2
item.text:
You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Thrown: If a weapon has the Thrown property, you can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack. If the weapon is a melee weapon, you use the same ability modifier for that attack roll and damage roll that you would use for a melee attack with the weapon. For example, if you throw a handaxe, you use your Strength, but if you throw a dagger, you can use either your Strength or your Dexterity, since the dagger has the Finesse property.
Range: A weapon that can be used to make a ranged attack has a range shown in parentheses after the ammunition or thrown property. The range lists two numbers. The first is the weapon's normal range in feet, and the second indicates the weapon's maximum range. When attacking a target beyond normal range, you have disadvantage on the attack roll. You can't attack a target beyond the weapon's long range.
Proficiency: simple, light hammer Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Longbow +2
item.text:
Ammo: arrow
You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Ammunition: You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. The ammunition of a firearm is destroyed upon use. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield. If you use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a melee attack, you treat the weapon as an improvised weapon (see "Improvised Weapons").
Range: A weapon that can be used to make a ranged attack has a range shown in parentheses after the ammunition or thrown property. The range lists two numbers. The first is the weapon's normal range in feet, and the second indicates the weapon's maximum range. When attacking a target beyond normal range, you have disadvantage on the attack roll. You can't attack a target beyond the weapon's long range.
Two-Handed: This weapon requires two hands when you attack with it.
Heavy: Creatures that are Small or Tiny have disadvantage on attack rolls with heavy weapons. A heavy weapon's size and bulk make it too large for a Small or Tiny creature to use effectively.
Proficiency: martial, longbow Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Longsword +2
item.text:
You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Versatile: This weapon can be used with one or two hands. A damage value in parentheses appears with the property—the damage when the weapon is used with two hands to make a melee attack.
Proficiency: martial, longsword Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Mace +2
item.text:
You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Proficiency: simple, mace Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Maul +2
item.text:
You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Heavy: Creatures that are Small or Tiny have disadvantage on attack rolls with heavy weapons. A heavy weapon's size and bulk make it too large for a Small or Tiny creature to use effectively.
Two-Handed: This weapon requires two hands when you attack with it.
Proficiency: martial, maul Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Morningstar +2
item.text:
You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Proficiency: martial, morningstar Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Net +2
item.text:
You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Special: A Large or smaller creature hit by a net is restrained until it is freed. A net has no effect on creatures that are formless, or creatures that are Huge or larger. A creature can use its action to make a DC 10 Strength check, freeing itself or another creature within its reach on a success. Dealing 5 slashing damage to the net (AC 10) also frees the creature without harming it, ending the effect and destroying the net. When you use an action, bonus action, or reaction to attack with a net, you can make only one attack regardless of the number of attacks you can normally make.
Thrown: If a weapon has the Thrown property, you can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack. If the weapon is a melee weapon, you use the same ability modifier for that attack roll and damage roll that you would use for a melee attack with the weapon. For example, if you throw a handaxe, you use your Strength, but if you throw a dagger, you can use either your Strength or your Dexterity, since the dagger has the Finesse property.
Range: A weapon that can be used to make a ranged attack has a range shown in parentheses after the ammunition or thrown property. The range lists two numbers. The first is the weapon's normal range in feet, and the second indicates the weapon's maximum range. When attacking a target beyond normal range, you have disadvantage on the attack roll. You can't attack a target beyond the weapon's long range.
Proficiency: martial, net Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Pike +2
item.text:
You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Heavy: Creatures that are Small or Tiny have disadvantage on attack rolls with heavy weapons. A heavy weapon's size and bulk make it too large for a Small or Tiny creature to use effectively.
Reach: This weapon adds 5 feet to your reach when you attack with it, as well as when determining your reach for opportunity attacks with it.
Two-Handed: This weapon requires two hands when you attack with it.
Proficiency: martial, pike Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Quarterstaff +2
item.text:
You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Versatile: This weapon can be used with one or two hands. A damage value in parentheses appears with the property—the damage when the weapon is used with two hands to make a melee attack.
Proficiency: simple, quarterstaff Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Rapier +2
item.text:
You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Finesse: When making an attack with a finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.
Proficiency: martial, rapier Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Scimitar +2
item.text:
You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.

item.name: Sling Bullets +3 (20)
item.text:
You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this piece of magic ammunition. Once it hits a target, the ammunition is no longer magical.
Ammunition: You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. The ammunition of a firearm is destroyed upon use. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Breastplate +3
item.text:
You have a +3 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.
This armor consists of a fitted metal chest piece worn with supple leather. Although it leaves the legs and arms relatively unprotected, this armor provides good protection for the wearer's vital organs while leaving the wearer relatively unencumbered.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Chain Mail +3
item.text:
You have a +3 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.
Made of interlocking metal rings, chain mail includes a layer of quilted fabric worn underneath the mail to prevent chafing and to cushion the impact of blows. The suit includes gauntlets.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
If the wearer has a Strength score lower than 13, their speed is reduced by 10 feet.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Chain Shirt +3
item.text:
You have a +3 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.
Made of interlocking metal rings, a chain shirt is worn between layers of clothing or leather. This armor offers modest protection to the wearer's upper body and allows the sound of the rings rubbing against one another to be muffled by outer layers.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Half Plate Armor +3
item.text:
You have a +3 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.
Half plate consists of shaped metal plates that cover most of the wearer's body. It does not include leg protection beyond simple greaves that are attached with leather straps.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Hide Armor +3
item.text:
You have a +3 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.
This crude armor consists of thick furs and pelts. It is commonly worn by barbarian tribes, evil humanoids, and other folk who lack access to the tools and materials needed to create better armor.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Leather Armor +3
item.text:
You have a +3 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.
The breastplate and shoulder protectors of this armor are made of leather that has been stiffened by being boiled in oil. The rest of the armor is made of softer and more flexible materials.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Padded Armor +3
item.text:
You have a +3 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.
Padded armor consists of quilted layers of cloth and batting.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Plate Armor +3
item.text:
You have a +3 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.
Plate consists of shaped, interlocking metal plates to cover the entire body. A suit of plate includes gauntlets, heavy leather boots, a visored helmet, and thick layers of padding underneath the armor. Buckles and straps distribute the weight over the body.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
If the wearer has a Strength score lower than 15, their speed is reduced by 10 feet.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Ring Mail +3
item.text:
You have a +3 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.
This armor is leather armor with heavy rings sewn into it. The rings help reinforce the armor against blows from swords and axes. Ring mail is inferior to chain mail, and it's usually worn only by those who can't afford better armor.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Scale Mail +3
item.text:
You have a +3 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.
This armor consists of a coat and leggings (and perhaps a separate skirt) of leather covered with overlapping pieces of metal, much like the scales of a fish. The suit includes gauntlets.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Splint Armor +3
item.text:
You have a +3 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.
This armor is made of narrow vertical strips of metal riveted to a backing of leather that is worn over cloth padding. Flexible chain mail protects the joints.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
If the wearer has a Strength score lower than 15, their speed is reduced by 10 feet.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Studded Leather Armor +3
item.text:
You have a +3 bonus to AC while wearing this armor.
Made from tough but flexible leather, studded leather is reinforced with close-set rivets or spikes.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Shield +3
item.text:
While holding this shield, you have a +3 bonus to AC. This bonus is in addition to the shield's normal bonus to AC.
A shield is made from wood or metal and is carried in one hand. Wielding a shield increases your Armor Class by 2. You can benefit from only one shield at a time.
Proficiency: shield Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Battleaxe +3
item.text:
You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Versatile: This weapon can be used with one or two hands. A damage value in parentheses appears with the property—the damage when the weapon is used with two hands to make a melee attack.
Proficiency: martial, battleaxe Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Blowgun +3
item.text:
Ammo: blowgun needle

Ammunition: You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. The ammunition of a firearm is destroyed upon use. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield. If you use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a melee attack, you treat the weapon as an improvised weapon (see "Improvised Weapons").
Range: A weapon that can be used to make a ranged attack has a range shown in parentheses after the ammunition or thrown property. The range lists two numbers. The first is the weapon's normal range in feet, and the second indicates the weapon's maximum range. When attacking a target beyond normal range, you have disadvantage on the attack roll. You can't attack a target beyond the weapon's long range.

If you use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a melee attack, you treat the weapon as an improvised weapon (see "Improvised Weapons").
Range: A weapon that can be used to make a ranged attack has a range shown in parentheses after the ammunition or thrown property. The range lists two numbers. The first is the weapon's normal range in feet, and the second indicates the weapon's maximum range. When attacking a target beyond normal range, you have disadvantage on the attack roll. You can't attack a target beyond the weapon's long range.
Loading: Because of the time required to load this weapon, you can fire only one piece of ammunition from it when you use an action, bonus action, or reaction to fire it, regardless of the number of attacks you can normally make.
Light: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.
Proficiency: martial, hand crossbow Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Lance +3
item.text:
You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Special: You have disadvantage when you use a lance to attack a target within 5 feet of you. Also, a lance requires two hands to wield when you aren't mounted.
Reach: This weapon adds 5 feet to your reach when you attack with it, as well as when determining your reach for opportunity attacks with it.
Proficiency: martial, lance Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Longsword +3
item.text:
You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Versatile: This weapon can be used with one or two hands. A damage value in parentheses appears with the property—the damage when the weapon is used with two hands to make a melee attack.
Proficiency: martial, longsword Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Mace +3
item.text:
You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Proficiency: simple, mace Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Maul +3
item.text:
You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

item.name: Morningstar +3
item.text:
You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Proficiency: martial, morningstar Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Net +3
item.text:
You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Special: A Large or smaller creature hit by a net is restrained until it is freed. A net has no effect on creatures that are formless, or creatures that are Huge or larger. A creature can use its action to make a DC 10 Strength check, freeing itself or another creature within its reach on a success. Dealing 5 slashing damage to the net (AC 10) also frees the creature without harming it, ending the effect and destroying the net. When you use an action, bonus action, or reaction to attack with a net, you can make only one attack regardless of the number of attacks you can normally make.
Thrown: If a weapon has the Thrown property, you can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack. If the weapon is a melee weapon, you use the same ability modifier for that attack roll and damage roll that you would use for a melee attack with the weapon. For example, if you throw a handaxe, you use your Strength, but if you throw a dagger, you can use either your Strength or your Dexterity, since the dagger has the Finesse property.
Range: A weapon that can be used to make a ranged attack has a range shown in parentheses after the ammunition or thrown property. The range lists two numbers. The first is the weapon's normal range in feet, and the second indicates the weapon's maximum range. When attacking a target beyond normal range, you have disadvantage on the attack roll. You can't attack a target beyond the weapon's long range.
Proficiency: martial, net Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.text:

Heavy: Creatures that are Small or Tiny have disadvantage on attack rolls with heavy weapons. A heavy weapon's size and bulk make it too large for a Small or Tiny creature to use effectively.

item.text:
You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Versatile: This weapon can be used with one or two hands. A damage value in parentheses appears with the property—the damage when the weapon is used with two hands to make a melee attack.
Proficiency: simple, quarterstaff Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Rapier +3
item.text:
You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Finesse: When making an attack with a finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.
Proficiency: martial, rapier Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Scimitar +3
item.text:
You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

item.name: War Pick +3
item.text:
You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Proficiency: martial, war pick Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Warhammer +3
item.text:
You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Versatile: This weapon can be used with one or two hands. A damage value in parentheses appears with the property—the damage when the weapon is used with two hands to make a melee attack.
Proficiency: martial, warhammer Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Whip +3
item.text:
You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
Finesse: When making an attack with a finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.
Reach: This weapon adds 5 feet to your reach when you attack with it, as well as when determining your reach for opportunity attacks with it.
Proficiency: martial, whip Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Breastplate of Acid Resistance
item.text:
You have resistance to acid damage while you wear this armor.
This armor consists of a fitted metal chest piece worn with supple leather. Although it leaves the legs and arms relatively unprotected, this armor provides good protection for the wearer's vital organs while leaving the wearer relatively unencumbered.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Chain Mail of Acid Resistance
item.text:
You have resistance to acid damage while you wear this armor.
Made of interlocking metal rings, chain mail includes a layer of quilted fabric worn underneath the mail to prevent chafing and to cushion the impact of blows. The suit includes gauntlets.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
If the wearer has a Strength score lower than 13, their speed is reduced by 10 feet.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Chain Shirt of Acid Resistance
item.text:
You have resistance to acid damage while you wear this armor.
Made of interlocking metal rings, a chain shirt is worn between layers of clothing or leather. This armor offers modest protection to the wearer's upper body and allows the sound of the rings rubbing against one another to be muffled by outer layers.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Half Plate Armor of Acid Resistance
item.text:
You have resistance to acid damage while you wear this armor.
Half plate consists of shaped metal plates that cover most of the wearer's body. It does not include leg protection beyond simple greaves that are attached with leather straps.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Hide Armor of Acid Resistance
item.text:
You have resistance to acid damage while you wear this armor.
This crude armor consists of thick furs and pelts. It is commonly worn by barbarian tribes, evil humanoids, and other folk who lack access to the tools and materials needed to create better armor.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Leather Armor of Acid Resistance
item.text:
You have resistance to acid damage while you wear this armor.
The breastplate and shoulder protectors of this armor are made of leather that has been stiffened by being boiled in oil. The rest of the armor is made of softer and more flexible materials.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Padded Armor of Acid Resistance
item.text:
You have resistance to acid damage while you wear this armor.
Padded armor consists of quilted layers of cloth and batting.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Plate Armor of Acid Resistance
item.text:
You have resistance to acid damage while you wear this armor.
Plate consists of shaped, interlocking metal plates to cover the entire body. A suit of plate includes gauntlets, heavy leather boots, a visored helmet, and thick layers of padding underneath the armor. Buckles and straps distribute the weight over the body.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
If the wearer has a Strength score lower than 15, their speed is reduced by 10 feet.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Ring Mail of Acid Resistance
item.text:
You have resistance to acid damage while you wear this armor.
This armor is leather armor with heavy rings sewn into it. The rings help reinforce the armor against blows from swords and axes. Ring mail is inferior to chain mail, and it's usually worn only by those who can't afford better armor.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Scale Mail of Acid Resistance
item.text:
You have resistance to acid damage while you wear this armor.
This armor consists of a coat and leggings (and perhaps a separate skirt) of leather covered with overlapping pieces of metal, much like the scales of a fish. The suit includes gauntlets.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Splint Armor of Acid Resistance
item.text:
You have resistance to acid damage while you wear this armor.
This armor is made of narrow vertical strips of metal riveted to a backing of leather that is worn over cloth padding. Flexible chain mail protects the joints.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
If the wearer has a Strength score lower than 15, their speed is reduced by 10 feet.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Studded Leather Armor of Acid Resistance
item.text:
You have resistance to acid damage while you wear this armor.
Made from tough but flexible leather, studded leather is reinforced with close-set rivets or spikes.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Breastplate of Cold Resistance
item.text:
You have resistance to cold damage while you wear this armor.
This armor consists of a fitted metal chest piece worn with supple leather. Although it leaves the legs and arms relatively unprotected, this armor provides good protection for the wearer's vital organs while leaving the wearer relatively unencumbered.

item.name: Padded Armor of Fire Resistance
item.text:
You have resistance to fire damage while you wear this armor.
Padded armor consists of quilted layers of cloth and batting.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Plate Armor of Fire Resistance
item.text:
You have resistance to fire damage while you wear this armor.
Plate consists of shaped, interlocking metal plates to cover the entire body. A suit of plate includes gauntlets, heavy leather boots, a visored helmet, and thick layers of padding underneath the armor. Buckles and straps distribute the weight over the body.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
If the wearer has a Strength score lower than 15, their speed is reduced by 10 feet.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Ring Mail of Fire Resistance
item.text:
You have resistance to fire damage while you wear this armor.
This armor is leather armor with heavy rings sewn into it. The rings help reinforce the armor against blows from swords and axes. Ring mail is inferior to chain mail, and it's usually worn only by those who can't afford better armor.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Scale Mail of Fire Resistance
item.text:
You have resistance to fire damage while you wear this armor.
This armor consists of a coat and leggings (and perhaps a separate skirt) of leather covered with overlapping pieces of metal, much like the scales of a fish. The suit includes gauntlets.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Splint Armor of Fire Resistance
item.text:
You have resistance to fire damage while you wear this armor.
This armor is made of narrow vertical strips of metal riveted to a backing of leather that is worn over cloth padding. Flexible chain mail protects the joints.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
If the wearer has a Strength score lower than 15, their speed is reduced by 10 feet.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Studded Leather Armor of Fire Resistance
item.text:
You have resistance to fire damage while you wear this armor.
Made from tough but flexible leather, studded leather is reinforced with close-set rivets or spikes.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Breastplate of Force Resistance
item.text:
You have resistance to force damage while you wear this armor.
This armor consists of a fitted metal chest piece worn with supple leather. Although it leaves the legs and arms relatively unprotected, this armor provides good protection for the wearer's vital organs while leaving the wearer relatively unencumbered.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Chain Mail of Force Resistance
item.text:
You have resistance to force damage while you wear this armor.
Made of interlocking metal rings, chain mail includes a layer of quilted fabric worn underneath the mail to prevent chafing and to cushion the impact of blows. The suit includes gauntlets.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
If the wearer has a Strength score lower than 13, their speed is reduced by 10 feet.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Chain Shirt of Force Resistance
item.text:
You have resistance to force damage while you wear this armor.
Made of interlocking metal rings, a chain shirt is worn between layers of clothing or leather. This armor offers modest protection to the wearer's upper body and allows the sound of the rings rubbing against one another to be muffled by outer layers.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Half Plate Armor of Force Resistance
item.text:
You have resistance to force damage while you wear this armor.
Half plate consists of shaped metal plates that cover most of the wearer's body. It does not include leg protection beyond simple greaves that are attached with leather straps.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Hide Armor of Force Resistance

This crude armor consists of thick furs and pelts. It is commonly worn by barbarian tribes, evil humanoids, and other folk who lack access to the tools and materials needed to create better armor.

item.name: Half Plate Armor of Thunder Resistance
item.text:
You have resistance to thunder damage while you wear this armor.
Half plate consists of shaped metal plates that cover most of the wearer's body. It does not include leg protection beyond simple greaves that are attached with leather straps.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Hide Armor of Thunder Resistance
item.text:
You have resistance to thunder damage while you wear this armor.
This crude armor consists of thick furs and pelts. It is commonly worn by barbarian tribes, evil humanoids, and other folk who lack access to the tools and materials needed to create better armor.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Leather Armor of Thunder Resistance
item.text:
You have resistance to thunder damage while you wear this armor.
The breastplate and shoulder protectors of this armor are made of leather that has been stiffened by being boiled in oil. The rest of the armor is made of softer and more flexible materials.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Padded Armor of Thunder Resistance
item.text:
You have resistance to thunder damage while you wear this armor.
Padded armor consists of quilted layers of cloth and batting.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Plate Armor of Thunder Resistance
item.text:
You have resistance to thunder damage while you wear this armor.
Plate consists of shaped, interlocking metal plates to cover the entire body. A suit of plate includes gauntlets, heavy leather boots, a visored helmet, and thick layers of padding underneath the armor. Buckles and straps distribute the weight over the body.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
If the wearer has a Strength score lower than 15, their speed is reduced by 10 feet.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Ring Mail of Thunder Resistance
item.text:
You have resistance to thunder damage while you wear this armor.
This armor is leather armor with heavy rings sewn into it. The rings help reinforce the armor against blows from swords and axes. Ring mail is inferior to chain mail, and it's usually worn only by those who can't afford better armor.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Scale Mail of Thunder Resistance
item.text:
You have resistance to thunder damage while you wear this armor.
This armor consists of a coat and leggings (and perhaps a separate skirt) of leather covered with overlapping pieces of metal, much like the scales of a fish. The suit includes gauntlets.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Splint Armor of Thunder Resistance
item.text:
You have resistance to thunder damage while you wear this armor.
This armor is made of narrow vertical strips of metal riveted to a backing of leather that is worn over cloth padding. Flexible chain mail protects the joints.
The wearer has disadvantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks.
If the wearer has a Strength score lower than 15, their speed is reduced by 10 feet.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Studded Leather Armor of Thunder Resistance
item.text:
You have resistance to thunder damage while you wear this armor.
Made from tough but flexible leather, studded leather is reinforced with close-set rivets or spikes.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

item.name: Arrow of Slaying
item.text:
An arrow of slaying is a magic weapon meant to slay a particular kind of creature. Some are more focused than others; for example, there are both arrows of dragon slaying and arrows of blue dragon slaying. If a creature belonging to the type, race, or group associated with an arrow of slaying takes damage from the arrow, the creature must make a DC 17 Constitution saving throw, taking an extra 6d10 piercing damage on a failed save, or half as much extra damage on a successful one. Once an arrow of slaying deals its extra damage to a creature, it becomes a nonmagical arrow.
Ammunition: You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. The ammunition of a firearm is destroyed upon use. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.
Source: System Reference Document 5.0

If a creature belonging to the type, race, or group associated with an arrow of slaying takes damage from the arrow, the creature must make a DC 17 Constitution saving throw, taking an extra 6d10 piercing damage on a failed save, or half as much extra damage on a successful one. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. The ammunition of a firearm is destroyed upon use.
